seasonArc: Puck Pieterse's 2026 was a season of near-misses at the top of women's racing, bookended by a cyclocross medal. She opened the winter with bronze at the UCI Cyclocross World Championships in Hulst (31 January), winning a final-lap fight for the last podium step behind an all-Dutch sweep led by Lucinda Brand. On the road she was a fixture in every classic finale: sixth at Strade Bianche (+16"), fourth in a tight Milano-Sanremo Donne sprint, then a run of podiums in the Ardennes and cobbled classics — third at the Ronde van Vlaanderen (+42"), second at La Flèche Wallonne (same time as the winner), and second again at Liège-Bastogne-Liège (+1:29). A spring that announced her as a complete one-day racer across every terrain, even if the biggest win stayed just out of reach.
